journal_entry.description: Acquisition of land with building for factory construction, financed by 6 annual instalments
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS, the land is recorded at the present value of the payment obligation ($49,173,240 calculated as $10,000,000 × 4.91732 annuity factor at 6% for 6 periods). The building to be demolished is not separately capitalized as it has no future economic benefit; its cost is included in land cost. The difference between the $60,000,000 face value and present value ($10,826,760) represents future interest expense that will be recognized over the payment period. The liability is recorded at present value per IAS 16 and IFRS 9. The existing building demolition cost is capitalized to land as it is necessary to prepare the asset for its intended use (factory construction site).

output.reason: Under IFRS, Ford is acquiring land (a non-current asset) for the purpose of constructing a new factory. The debit side records the acquisition of the land asset at its present value. The existing building to be demolished is part of the land acquisition cost (demolition costs are capitalized to land under IFRS). This represents 1 asset increase (Land or Property under development). The financing arrangement (instalments payable) affects only the credit side of the entry.

debit_classifier.reason: Under IFRS, Ford is acquiring land (a non-current asset) for the purpose of constructing a new factory. The debit side records the acquisition of the land asset at its present value. The existing building to be demolished is part of the land acquisition cost (demolition costs are capitalized to land under IFRS). This represents 1 asset increase (Land or Property under development). The financing arrangement (instalments payable) affects only the credit side of the entry.

output.reason: This transaction involves acquiring land with a building for $60,000,000 to be paid in instalments. On the credit side: (1) Cash is paid immediately for any down payment or initial costs - but the problem states payments commence at the END of the year, so no immediate cash outflow occurs at acquisition. (2) A liability is created for the deferred payment obligation. Under IFRS, the $60,000,000 payable in instalments must be recorded at present value, creating a financial liability (notes payable/long-term debt). This represents a liability increase. Since no immediate cash payment is made at acquisition (payments start at year-end), there is only one credit line: the liability increase for the present value of the payment obligation.

Long-term Debt credited for same amount, creating proper liability at present value. Entry balances (debit = credit). The $10,826,760 difference between face value ($60,000,000) and present value will be recognized as interest expense over the payment period using effective interest method. All accounts appropriate, amounts reasonable, and no lines missing.
